summary.title: QRScope Threat Model
summary.description: Browser-based QR code inspection utility that decodes QR codes from uploads, screenshots, or camera input before the user decides whether to open the result.

text.text:
External Destination URL 
Outside QRScope Control

data.name:
External Destination URL 
Outside QRScope Control
data.reasonOutOfScope: QRScope may show the decoded link, but it does not control the destination website.

threats.title: User trusts a spoofed QR destination
threats.description: A QR code may point to a spoofed or lookalike destination that imitates a trusted website, brand, login page, payment page, or university service. Because QR codes hide the destination from the user until decoded, the user may trust the QR code and open a malicious link without recognizing the risk.
threats.mitigation: Display the decoded QR content before navigation, show the full destination clearly, avoid automatic link opening, defang suspicious URLs when appropriate, and require deliberate user action before leaving QRScope.

threats.title: Oversized or malformed image causes browser performance issue
threats.description: A user may upload an oversized, malformed, or unsupported QR image that causes excessive browser resource usage, decoder errors, slow performance, or a failed scan. Because QRScope processes user-provided images in the browser, malformed input should be handled safely without freezing the page or creating repeated decode attempts.
threats.mitigation: Enforce file size limits, allow only supported image types, validate file MIME/type before processing, handle decoder errors safely, prevent repeated decode loops, and display a clear error message when an image cannot be processed.

threats.title: Camera scan continues after successful decode
threats.description:
Threat: Camera scan continues after successful decode
Category: Information Disclosure / Denial of Service
Mitigation: Stop scanning after a successful decode and require the user to start a new scan manually.
threats.mitigation: Stop scanning after a successful decode and require the user to start a new scan manually.

threats.title: Malicious QR content rendered unsafely
threats.description: A QR code may contain crafted text, HTML-like content, JavaScript-like payloads, or misleading URL formatting intended to manipulate how decoded content is displayed to the user. If QRScope renders decoded content unsafely, an attacker could cause script execution, visual deception, or unsafe link presentation inside the browser.
threats.mitigation: Render decoded QR content as plain text only. Escape all user-controlled output, avoid unsafe HTML rendering, do not use dangerouslySetInnerHTML, do not auto-open decoded links, and require deliberate user action before navigation.

data.name: Browser Memory / Temporary State
data.description: No database, no backend API, no QRScope API, no persistent upload storage

threats.title: Sensitive screenshot data exposed through persistence
threats.description: Uploaded QR screenshots may contain sensitive information outside the QR code, such as messages, account details, names, emails, location data, or other private screen content. If uploaded images or decoded QR contents were stored, cached, logged, or exposed beyond the browser session, sensitive user information could be disclosed.
threats.mitigation: Process uploaded QR images in the browser, avoid persistent storage, do not save uploaded images or decoded QR contents, clear temporary browser state when the user scans another QR code, and clearly disclose the no-storage behavior to users.

data.name: Grants camera access / scans QR code
data.description: Grants camera access / scans QR code

labels: Grants camera access / scans QR code
